LAW AND MOTION 302, EFENDANTS NATHANIEL LANDS, WILLIAM COTTON AND QUICKCOIN, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY THE PROCEEDINGS UNDER CALIFORNIA CIVIL PROCEDURE CODE SECTION 1281 - THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED AND THE COURT STAYS THIS ACTION PURSUANT TO CCP 1281.4. DEFENDANTS SUFFICIENTLY ESTABLISH THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT AND PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DISPUTE THE EXISTENCE OF THE AGREEMENT. SEE CONDEE V. LONGWOOD MGMT. CORP. (2001) 88 CAL.APP.4TH 215. THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE IS BROAD ENOUGH TO APPLY TO ALL OF THE CITED DISPUTES BETWEEN THE PARTIES. "[A]NY DOUBTS CONCERNING THE SCOPE OF ARBITRABLE ISSUES SHOULD BE RESOLVED IN FAVOR OF ARBITRATION." CRONUS INVESTMENTS, INC. V. CONCIERGE SERVS. (2005) 35 CAL.4TH 376, 384. THE DECLARATION OF DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL (MR. YURASEK) AT PAR. 5 INDICATES THAT DEFENDANTS DEMANDED ARBITRATION IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 1281.2. LASTLY, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY SHOW THAT HE WAS FRAUDULENTLY INDUCED INTO AGREEING TO ARBITRATE HIS DISPUTES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST J. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-01-2015 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-04-2014 Continued To May-20-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Proceedings Under California Civil Procedure Code Section 1281; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. EUGENE L. HAHM OF LTL ATTORNEYS, TEL. NO. (650)241-2140 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARA BOUNDY & ATTY. JASON A. YURASEK OF THE PERKINS & COIE LLP, TEL. NO. 344-7173 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS NATHANIEL LANDS, WILLIAM COTTON and QUICKCOIN, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Proceedings Under California Civil Procedure Code Section 1281 - The motion to compel arbitration is granted and the court stays this action pursuant to CCP 1281.4. Defendants sufficiently establish the arbitration agreement and plaintiff does not dispute the existence of the agreement. See Condee v. Longwood Mgmt. Corp. (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 215. The arbitration clause is broad enough to apply to all of the cited disputes between the parties. "[A]ny doubts concerning the scope of arbitrable issues should be resolved in favor of arbitration." Cronus Investments, Inc. v. Concierge Servs. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 376, 384. The declaration of defendants' counsel (Mr. Yurasek) at par. 5 indicates that defendants demanded arbitration in compliance with CCP 1281.2. Lastly, plaintiff does not sufficiently show that he was fraudulently induced into agreeing to arbitrate his disputes. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
